plant_constituents.name: apigenin
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita apigenin is a flavonoid compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits. Studies have shown that apigenin may have a role in reducing anxiety and stress, as well as exerting neuroprotective effects. Additionally, apigenin has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in wound healing and infection prevention.

plant_constituents.name: volatile oils
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita volatile oils, primarily consisting of azulene and sesquiterpenes, possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them beneficial for soothing skin irritations and calming muscle spasms. These volatile oils have also been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can aid in the treatment of skin infections and wound healing. The calming effects of chamomilla recutita volatile oils have made them a popular ingredient in skincare products and herbal remedies.

plant_constituents.name: bisabolol
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita bisabolol is a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been traditionally used to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and burns, and has shown promise in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Bisabolol's antioxidant properties also contribute to its potential in protecting against oxidative stress and promoting wound healing.

plant_constituents.name: chamazulene
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita chamazulene is a blue-colored, oily medicinal active constituent that is primarily responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and other skin irritations. Studies have shown that chamazulene also possesses anti-cancer and neuroprotective properties, making it a promising area of research in the field of pharmacology.

plant_constituents.name: terpenoids
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita terpenoids are a group of bioactive compounds found in chamomile, contributing to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. These terpenoids work by interacting with the body's neurotransmitters and hormones, helping to reduce stress and anxiety. The primary terpenoid in chamomile is apigenin, which has been shown to have a sedative effect and promote relaxation.

plant_constituents.name: ursolic acid
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita ursolic acid is a triterpenoid compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits. Research has shown that ursolic acid exhibits immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system and mitigate inflammation. Its anti-cancer properties have also been investigated, with some studies indicating that ursolic acid may inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis.

plant_constituents.name: flavonoids
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita flavonoids have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to their potential health benefits. These flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to have a positive impact on cardiovascular health and may also exhibit antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Additionally, flavonoids present in chamomilla recutita may help in soothing and calming the digestive system, reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.

plant_constituents.name: triterpenes
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita triterpenes have been identified as a key component with potential therapeutic benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These triterpenes are thought to contribute to the plant's soothing and calming effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Research on chamomilla triterpenes continues to explore their efficacy in treating conditions such as anxiety and skin irritations.

plant_constituents.name: waxy substances
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita waxy substances, known as triterpene saponins, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to the herb's soothing effects on the skin and digestive system. These compounds also exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, further highlighting their potential in medicinal applications. The triterpene saponins in chamomilla recutita may also help to protect the skin from damage and promote wound healing.

plant_constituents.name: farnesene
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita farnesene, a sesquiterpene compound, has been identified as a key player in the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of chamomile. Farnesene is believed to contribute to the plant's ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, making it a valuable component in traditional herbal remedies. Research suggests that farnesene may also have potential as a natural preservative due to its antimicrobial properties.

plant_constituents.name: glycosides
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita glycosides, specifically apigenin and luteolin, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to their potential therapeutic benefits. These glycosides have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to soothe and calm various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin irritations. The anti-inflammatory effects of chamomilla glycosides have also been studied for their potential in treating conditions like eczema and psoriasis.

plant_constituents.name: luteolin
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita luteolin is a flavonoid compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential therapeutic benefits. Research has shown that luteolin in chamomilla recutita may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can lead to various health issues. Additionally, luteolin has been found to have neuroprotective and antimicrobial properties, further solidifying its importance in traditional herbal medicine.

plant_constituents.name: eudesmol
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita eudesmol, a sesquiterpene compound, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits. Research has shown that eudesmol can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Additionally, eudesmol has been studied for its potential to modulate the immune system and exert antimicrobial effects.

plant_constituents.name: isofraxidin
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita isofraxidin is a bioactive flavonoid found in the flowers of the chamomile plant. Isofraxidin has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. It is also believed to have a positive effect on the immune system and may have therapeutic applications in the treatment of various conditions.

plant_constituents.name: saponins
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita saponins are a group of bioactive compounds found in the flowers of the chamomile plant, known for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research suggests that these saponins may contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects, including soothing skin irritations and alleviating anxiety. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the mechanisms and benefits of saponins in chamomile.

plant_constituents.name: methanol
plant_constituents.desc: Chamomilla recutita methanol, a key component of chamomile extract, has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. Research has also suggested that methanol from chamomile may have a potential role in antioxidant activity, helping to protect cells from oxidative stress. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal effects of methanol in chamomile.
